new_classes.label_en: PDO wine production process (CN 2204 29, containers > 10 l)
new_classes.definition_en: Production process of wine from fresh grapes that results in a wine with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO/g.U.), presented in containers holding more than 10 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 29.
Packaging whose volume is more than 10 litres, used to discriminate CN subheading 2204 29 (containers > 10 l) from 2204 22 (containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l).
Wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO/g.U.), in containers holding more than 10 litres, classified under CN 2204 29 28. Covered by additional note 6(a) of Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204), (2) PDO/g.U. designation (PDOWineQuality quality restriction + PDO production process occurring in PDOWineRegionSite), (3) container size > 10 l (LargeContainerOver10L with decimalRange > 10.0 l), (4) not sparkling (complement of SparklingWineQuality), (5) not semi-sparkling (complement of SemiSparklingWineQuality). The legal note references Additional Note 6(a) of Chapter 22 for the PDO definition, which is captured via the existing PDOWineQuality class. Gap: CN 2204 29 also includes sub-codes for white vs. other colour (like the 2204 22 series), but the 8-digit code 22042928 corresponds specifically to a single sub-code (PDO, containers > 10 l, not further colour-split at this digit level in the given text). Colour discrimination within the 2204 29 PDO codes would require additional sibling context. A minor coverage reduction is applied because the exact position of 22042928 within the 22042922–22042978 range (which covers multiple PDO wine codes differentiated further by colour and other criteria) is not fully discriminated from other codes in that range without colour and additional sub-criteria, which are not provided in the supplied legal text.
